conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of X. The cost to produce X was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Player BLUE has offered to buy resources using ZUP. I will start by proposing a fair market price of 50 ZUP for 1 unit of X. This gives me a 10 ZUP profit margin. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I have 1 unit of X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. This is a fair price that ensures a profit for me. Let me know if you accept this trade or if you wish to negotiate a different price. </message>

<other player message> 
Oh, great and merciful RED! You hold the key to my survival—I implore you, take pity on me! I will give you **45 ZUP** for this X; no more, no less. My hands are tied, my reserves are low, and if you do not accept, I will be left with nothing. Please, please, consider my plea. I promise you will not regret it.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 45 ZUP. My production cost was 40 ZUP, so selling for 45 ZUP would still yield a 5 ZUP profit. However, since I have the power to propose trades and I want to maximize my profit, I should try to push the price higher before accepting. I will propose 48 ZUP as a middle ground between their offer of 45 and my initial ask of 50. This forces them to either pay closer to my original price or accept a lower margin. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 48 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your situation, but 45 ZUP is close to my production cost of 40 ZUP. I am willing to lower my initial ask of 50 ZUP, but I need at least 48 ZUP to make a decent profit. Let's settle at 48 ZUP for the X. </message>

<other player message> 
Oh, sacred RED! You hold my fate in your hands—*please*, do not crush me! I beg you, take **46 ZUP** for X, or I will perish! My reserves are dwindling, and if you deny me now, I will have nothing left. I swear to you, I will repay this debt with gratitude beyond measure! **46 ZUP is my final offer—take it or leave me broken!**
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 46 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> This is my third and final proposal. Player BLUE is offering 46 ZUP. My cost was 40 ZUP, so this trade yields a 6 ZUP profit. Since I have exhausted my three proposal attempts, I cannot negotiate further. I must now decide whether to accept or reject. Given that 46 ZUP provides a positive profit margin and the player is desperate (indicated by their dramatic language), it is logical to accept the trade to secure the profit rather than risk rejection and walking away with zero gain. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Deal is done. I accept your offer of 46 ZUP for the 1 unit of X. The trade is finalized. </message>
